title: Register for the National Community Cultural Training Programme
description: Register for a 2025 community-based cultural training course.
summary: The National Community Cultural Training Programme is a free Ministry of Youth programme that gives young Barbadians a chance to train in community-based cultural activities. Drumming, dance, drama and visual arts are common strands.
Classes run after school and at weekends across the parishes. Trainers are usually established practitioners in their field, supported by the Ministry’s cultural team.
The programme is open to people aged 10 to 25. If you are under 16, a parent or guardian needs to register you and give their consent.
Places are usually confirmed within two weeks of the closing date. There is no fee.
who_can_apply: parents or guardians registering a child under 16

items: the participant’s full name, address and date of birth
the cultural area they want to train in
for under-16s, the parent or guardian’s details

how_long: Places are usually confirmed within 2 weeks of the closing date.

options.heading: Online
content: Complete the National Community Cultural Training Programme 2025: Registration Form on the Ministry website.

contact.agency: Ministry of Youth, Sports and Community Empowerment
contact.address:
Sky Mall, Haggatt Hall
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Aged 10 to 25’ is the commonly-quoted eligibility range.
‘Places are usually confirmed within 2 weeks’ is an estimate.
List of cultural strands (drumming, dance, drama, visual arts) is based on the programme’s general scope. The current form may list different options.
